summary: Synthesis-boundary review accepts the candidate. The candidate creates PubMed EFetch source snapshots and attaches source_snapshot_id provenance to existing DKD, IPF, and AD-risk D+Q records without expanding endpoint claims, upgrading certainty, or converting abstract/triage-derived records into full-article extraction.

findings.claim_or_issue: The proposed change is limited to PubMed EFetch snapshot creation and source_snapshot_id provenance attachment.
findings.why_it_matters: A provenance repair should not itself create new efficacy conclusions or broaden the synthesis basis beyond metadata and abstract-supported evidence.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required; retain the provenance repair as metadata/abstract-only support.

findings.claim_or_issue: The reviewed finding and result records preserve low-confidence, pilot-study caveats: DKD remains human biomarker evidence, IPF remains feasibility/tolerability and underpowered, and AD-risk remains single-arm exploratory functional evidence.
findings.why_it_matters: These boundaries prevent the source-snapshot provenance repair from being interpreted as stronger clinical, disease-modifying, or general longevity evidence.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required; keep the existing caveats and low-confidence classifications.

findings.claim_or_issue: Legacy IPF outcome, result, finding, and risk-of-bias records that remain derived or triage-summary records continue to state that full source-located extraction is pending.
findings.why_it_matters: Attaching a PubMed EFetch snapshot should not erase the distinction between abstract-supported provenance and full article/table extraction.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required in this lane; any later certainty upgrade should wait for source-located extraction.
